title: Co-Designing Parkinson’s Care: Hybrid Open House Outcomes
authors_full: Denise Stats-Caldwell, M.A., CCC-SLP; Elizabeth Trueba, M.S., CCC-SLP; Ileana Ratiu, Ph.D., CCC-SLP

summary.hook: This poster evaluates a Parkinson’s disease (PD) community education “open house” delivered in a hybrid format (in-person at ASU Tempe campus clinic and live via Zoom).
summary.summary_30s: This poster evaluates a Parkinson’s disease (PD) community education “open house” delivered in a hybrid format (in-person at ASU Tempe campus clinic and live via Zoom). The project aimed to describe how people with PD, care partners, and professionals perceived the event and to identify benefits, unmet needs, and priorities for future education initiatives.

audience_versions.clinician: This poster evaluates a Parkinson’s disease (PD) community education “open house” delivered in a hybrid format (in-person at ASU Tempe campus clinic and live via Zoom). Approximately 50 individuals attended the event. For clinicians, the main value is whether the findings could inform care decisions, screening, workflow, or patient communication.
audience_versions.community: This poster evaluates a Parkinson’s disease (PD) community education “open house” delivered in a hybrid format (in-person at ASU Tempe campus clinic and live via Zoom). Community-based education events may improve resource awareness and therapy familiarity for people with PD and care partners. For community partners, the key question is who benefits, who might be left out, and how the work could translate into real-world...

general: What is the main takeaway you hope attendees remember from this poster?

Why does this work matter beyond this specific poster session?
undergrad: What part of this project would be most helpful for an undergraduate student to learn from?

grad: How did you design the 7 interactive stations—what learning objectives did each station target?

How did the hybrid delivery work in practice (staffing for Zoom vs in-person, station flow, participant movement)?
